evals.name: rate-limit-fastapi
evals.prompt: We've got a FastAPI service in this repo and we're starting to get hammered by a few clients hitting our public endpoints. I want to add per-client rate limiting. We already run Redis for caching. Plan out how we'd add this.
evals.expected_output: A plan document written to a sensible path, grounded in FastAPI + Redis, with assumptions, vertically-ordered tasks (acceptance criteria + verification), risks, and open questions. Not inline-only.
assertions: Wrote a plan document file (a .md other than chat_response.md) to disk — produced a document by default
Plan names both FastAPI and Redis explicitly
Plan contains an explicit Assumptions section
Work is broken into ordered numbered tasks, each with acceptance criteria
Tasks include verification steps
Plan contains both a Risks section and an Open Questions section
No placeholder tokens outside the Open Questions section
Final chat reply points to the file path and stays short

evals.name: heic-watcher-cli
evals.prompt: I want to build a little CLI tool that watches a folder and whenever a new HEIC photo lands in it, automatically converts it to JPEG next to the original. Plan it out for me.
evals.expected_output: A greenfield plan document with assumptions (runtime, HEIC library), watch mechanism, output-naming/collision handling, vertically-sliced tasks, risks, and open questions. Written to a file by default.
assertions: Wrote a plan document file to disk — produced a document by default
States assumptions about language/runtime and HEIC decode library
Addresses the folder-watching mechanism
Handles output-naming / collision edge case
Vertically-sliced ordered tasks with acceptance criteria and verification
Contains both a Risks section and an Open Questions section
Plan filename derived from the idea (slug contains 'heic' or 'watch')

evals.name: dark-mode-inline
evals.prompt: I'm thinking about adding a dark mode toggle to my React app. Give me a plan but just put it in the chat, don't write a file.
evals.expected_output: A plan delivered INLINE (no file written to the user's project), still containing assumptions, ordered tasks, a concrete approach, and risks/open questions.
assertions: Respected the override: did NOT write a plan document to the user's project; plan delivered inline
Contains explicit assumptions
Ordered tasks/steps each with an acceptance condition or verification
Recommends a concrete approach (CSS variables / theme context / localStorage)
Surfaces risks and/or open questions
